title: Tetrapanax ricinifolius
intro: Tetrapanax ricinifolius, also known as the rice daphne or fairy fans, is a plant species in the family Araliaceae, native to Taiwan and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrapanax ricinifolius include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetrapanax ricinifolius is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and also used to make topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Tetrapanax ricinifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is native to China and Taiwan, and is also known as the "rice daphne" or "four-leaf elephant ear". The plant is a shrub or small tree that can grow up to 3-6 meters in height. Its large leaves can reach up to 1 meter in diameter, and are typically a bright green color. As a member of the Araliaceae family, Tetrapanax ricinifolius is closely related to other plants such as the sassafras and the ivy.
